notes: first truck just arrived, 7 oh 5. Hanson concrete, 6 cubes of 32 MPA with 100 slump. docket number is H 445821. driver was Pete
second concrete truck rolling in now at 7:20. another 6 cubes same spec. docket is H 445822
third and final concrete truck for the morning pour. quarter to 8. 4 cubes this time. docket H 445823. so thats 16 cubes total which is spot on what we ordered
Boral timber truck showed up at 8:30. 2 packs of 90 by 45 H2 treated pine, 100 lengths total at 5.4 metres. delivery note is BT 98234. all straight no rejects

plumbing supplies from Reece arrived about quarter past 9. 30 metres of 15 mil copper pipe, 20 metres of 20 mil copper, box of assorted fittings, and 2 Rinnai hot water units. checked against the order and one fitting pack is missing. we ordered 3 packs of 15 mil elbows and only got 2. called Reece theyre sending the missing pack this arvo
the missing elbow pack from Reece arrived at 2pm. all sorted now
steel arrived from OneSteel at 10. 3 tonne of reo bar, mix of N12 N16 and N20. all tagged with heat numbers matching the mill certs. unloaded it with the crane into the reo laydown area

insulation batts from Fletcher at 11:30. 40 packs of R2.5 wall batts and 20 packs of R4.0 ceiling batts. stored em in the container to keep dry. thats the full insulation order for the whole job which is good

brickworks truck at 1pm. 6 pallets of face bricks PGH dry pressed in Limestone. about 480 per pallet so just under 3000 bricks. we only ordered 2500 so weve got a few hundred spare which is fine, better too many than too few

electrical gear from Middys at half 2. 5 rolls of 2.5 mil TPS, 5 rolls of 1.5 mil TPS, 3 switchboards, about 80 GPOs, 40 light switches, and a box of miscellaneous bits. the sparky checked it all says its complete

roofing materials from Stratco at 3. 35 sheets of custom orb colorbond in monument colour, each sheet is 6 metres long. also 4 boxes of roofing screws and 2 rolls of sisalation. all looks good. stacked the sheets on bearers off the ground with the protective film still on
rejected delivery: a pallet of tiles showed up from Beaumont tiles but nobody ordered them. wrong site. sent the driver away. not our problem

running total of materials on site now: we've got enough bricks for about 2 more weeks, enough timber to frame level 1 completely, full insulation order stored, full electrical rough-in materials, and roofing materials ready to go once framing is done
materials we still need to order: guttering and downpipes, external cladding, internal plasterboard, doors and frames, and all the finishing hardware. the PM is sorting the orders this week
